content_en:
> Part of a lengthy supplication reported by Ahmad [3712], Abii ¥a‘la [q 1/156], at-Tabarani in a/-Kabir [3/74/1] and others. The hadith is sahib. Refer to as-Sabibah [no. 199]. The wording of this hadith of Ahmad, however, is in a singular gender not plural. [Translator’s Note]

Allah, the Exalted said,

Aare

SNS eRe Ste 5 RAGS see W354: BND 16

The example of those who disbelieve, is as that of him who shouts to the (flock of sheep) that hears nothing but calls and cries. (They are) deaf, dumb and blind.

[seerah al-Baqarah (2) : 171]

RAE TA CaA i o ae

pest see aged

And among them are some who listen to you, but can you make the deaf hear, even though they comprehend not? And among them are some who look at you, but can you guide the blind, even though they see not?

[Sarab Yanus (10) : 42-43]

ebes3 AE thes He dZat, spols 15155 pelSNe52 Mandl ES iy. ig YG oHI BAS GEERT CW GLATY,

And of them are some who listen to you; but We have set veils on their hearts, so they understand it not, and deafness in their ears; if they

[1.8 The State of the Dead Heart]

see every one of the signs they will not believe therein, to the point that when they come to argue with you, the disbelievers say: “These are nothing but tales of the men of old.”

[Sarah al-An‘Gm (6) : 25]

So He informed us that their hearts cannot undefstand, and their ears cannot heat, and they do not believe in what they have seen of the Fire as He informed us about them when He said,

aK WEL IL res xetaceiss Zope Cece PBZ ey we ASOD ASAD LL s Giles sdf ects And they say: ‘Our hearts are under coverings from that which you invite us to, and in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a screen.’ [Surah Fussilat (41) : 5]

So they mentioned the bartiers upon their hearts, ears and eyes. Their bodies are alive - heating and seeing, but this is a life of the body devoid of life in the heart - like the life of an animal - for the animals possess hearing and seeing, and eat and drink and marry.

This is why Allah said,

Fea gee

The example of those who disbelieve, is as that of him who shouts to the (flock of sheep) that heats nothing but calls and cries.

[Sarab al-Bagarah (2) : 171}

Likening them to the cattle, at whom the shepherd shouts, and they hear nothing except the cry (not understanding what is said), as He said in other verses,

rae “C2 404

Yhjahess pipes newts Orcas ce

Or do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are only like cattle - nay, they are even farther astray from the Path.

[Sarah al-Furgan (25) : 44]

Sb Hiciieee Hesths ith z ssid aN NEA tetera

oh fe 7%

GDL sass (Padsijatch Lidikasdt,

And indeed We have created many of the jinn and Mankind for Hell. They have hearts wherewith they understand not, they have eyes wherewith they hear not. They are like cattle - nay even more astray...

Steg

[seerah al-Araf (1) : 179]

A group of the commentators, when referring to these verses and those that resembled them such as the verse,

als ESE Gc hea G03 Gc

oes Boe @ PAGS Bor Lepassigcies And when harm touches man, he invokes Us,

lying down on his side, or sitting or standing. But when We have removed his harm from him,

[1.8 The State of the Dead Heart]

he passes on his way as if he has never invoked Us for a harm that touched him! [Sarah Yunus (10) : 12]

Regarding these and othet such verses that mention the faults of man and their condemnation, (the commentators) said,

These verses refer to the disbelievers, and that the meaning of ‘man’ here is ‘the disbelievers.’

So the one who hears this explanation is left thinking that one who openly manifests Islam is not included in this condemnation and threat, rather his thoughts link (these verses) to those who openly manifested shirk from amongst the Arabs, ot to those he knows to have openly shown disbelief such as the Jews, the Christians and the polytheists of Turkey and India - and hence he would not benefit from these verses that Allah revealed so that His servants may be guided,

So it is said in reply that firstly: those that openly manifest Islam include amongst them the believer and hypocrite, and the hypocrites are many in all periods of time and they are in the lowest level of the Hellfire.

Secondly: man possesses a strain of hypocrisy and disbelief even if he possesses faith along with this, as the Prophet (8) said in the fadith reported by both al-Bukhari and Muslim,

There are four qualities which if found in a person make him a pure hypocrite, and the one who has a portion of them has a portion of hypocrisy until he leaves them: when he speaks he lies, when he is entrusted, he betrays, when he speaks, he speak a lie, when he makes a covenant, he proves treacherous,

when he quarrels, he behaves in a evil and insulting mannet °

So he informed us that the one who has a portion of these then he has a portion of hypocrisy, and it is established in the Sabib of al-Bukhari that he said to Aba Dhar,

Indeed you have displayed a trait of Jahiliyyah in

you,’

And Abi Dhar was from the most truthful of people with respect to his faith. He (4%) said in the authentic ahadith,

Four (traits) in my Ummah are from the matters of Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic ignorance): boasting about noble descent, abusing the lineage, wailing [over the dead] and seeking rain from the stars. *

You will indeed follow the ways of those that came before you, inch by inch such that if they were to enter a lizard hole, you too would do so. They asked,

° Sabih al-Bukhari (Eng. Trans, 1/32 no. 33,] and Sahih Muslim (Eng, Trans. 1/40 no. 111).

7 Referring to the hadith reported by al-Ma‘rar that, ‘At ar-Rabadha | met Abii Dhik (radiyAllahu ‘anha), who was wearing a cloak and likewise his slave. asked about the reason for this. He replied, ‘I abused a person by calling his mother ‘with bad name,’ The Prophet (4) said to me, ‘O Abii Dhar! Did you abuse him by calling his mother with bad names? You have displayed .a trait of Jahiliyyah. Your slaves are yout brothers and Allah has placed them under your command. So whoever has a brother under his command should feed him of what he eats and dress him of what he wears. Do not ask slaves to do things beyond their capacity and if you do so, then help them.’ Sahih al-Bukhari [Eng, Trans. 1/29 no, 29] [Translator’s Note]

* Sabih Muslim (Eng, Trans. 2/444 no. 2033}.

[1.8 The State of the Dead Heart]

‘Do you mean the Jews and Christians?’ He replied, ‘Who else?”

What the early nations took to shall also be taken to by my nation, cubit by cubit and handspan by handspan. They said, ‘Do you mean the Persians and the Romans.’ To which he teplied, ‘Who else from amongst the people could it be.”"”

Ibn Abi Mulaykah said,

T met thirty of the Companions of Muhammad (3) all of them fearing hypocrisy for themselves. "

And from ‘Ali - or Hudhayfah - that he said,

The heart ate of four types: the clear heart that is illuminated by a torch - this is the heart of the believer. The encased heart - this is the heart of the disbeliever, the inverted heart - this is the heart of the hypocrite, and the heart that has two attractions, a time when it is called to faith, and a time when it is called to hypoctisy - these are a people that have mixed good actions with evil ones.

So when this is understood, it becomes known that every setvant benefits from what Allah mentioned concetning faith, either extolling the branches of faith or censuring the branches of disbelief.

° Sabib al-Bukhari (Eng, Trans. 9/314 no. 422], Sahib Muslim (Eng, Trans. 4/1402 no. 6448], Ahmad [2/450].

” Refer to Lgtidd Sirat al-Mustagim of Ibn Taymiyyah.

" Reported by al-Bukhari and Muslim

The case mentioned above is similar to what some of them ask concerning His saying,

Opes tai Guide us to the Straight Path. [Sarah al-Fatibah (1): 6]

saying: ‘Allah has already guided the believer, so what benefit is there in seeking guidance?’ Then some of them reply by saying that the meaning is ‘keep us firm upon guidance’ as the Arab would say to the one who is asleep, ‘sleep until I come to you.’ Others from amongst them say that the meaning is, ‘keep our hearts firm upon the guidance’ and that the request for firmness has been omitted. Yet others from amongst them say that it means, ‘increase me in guidance.’

This question really occurs due to the absence of their contemplating upon the Straight Path to which the servant seeks guidance to, for the meaning [of the verse] is [seeking guidance to] act according to what Allah ordered, and leave what He forbade in all matters.

[1.9 The Need for Beneficial Knowledge]

This is because the person, even if he has believed that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah and that the Quran is the truth in a general way, is commonly in need of knowledge of that which would benefit him and harm him. He is in need of knowledge concerning what he has been commanded to do and forbidden from doing in the finer aspects of the matters and in those areas of which he has no knowledge. [Not only this but we find that] that which he does have knowledge of, he does not put the greater part of it to practice! Assuming that all of

[1.9 The Need for Beneficial Knowledge]

the commands and prohibitions contained in the Qur'an and Sunnah have reached him, then the Qut’an and Sunnah contain laws that are general and universal for which it is not possible to specify to every individual person - therefore the person has been commanded due to the likes of this to ask for guidance to the Straight Path.

Guidance to the Straight Path includes all of the following matters: cognizance of what the Messenger (gg) came with in detail, cognizance of what comes under his general orders and concern for acting according to ones knowledge, for indeed just having knowledge is not a cause for attaining guidance if one does not act according to his knowledge. This is why He said to His Prophet after the treaty of Hudaybiyyah,
content_id:
Bagian dari doa yang panjang yang diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, Abu Ya'la, Ath-Thabarani, dan lainnya. Hadis ini sahih.

Allah ﷻ Yang Mahatinggi berfirman:

"Perumpamaan orang-orang kafir itu adalah seperti pengembala yang memanggil binatang yang tidak mendengar selain panggilan dan seruan saja. Mereka tuli, bisu, dan buta, maka tidaklah mereka mengerti." [Surah Al-Baqarah (2): 171]

"Dan di antara mereka ada orang yang mendengarkan perkataanmu, tetapi apakah kamu dapat menjadikan orang-orang tuli itu mendengar, walaupun mereka tidak mempergunakan akalnya? Dan di antara mereka ada orang yang memandang kepadamu, tetapi apakah kamu dapat memberi petunjuk kepada orang-orang buta, walaupun mereka tidak mempergunakan penglihatannya?" [Surah Yunus (10): 42-43]

"Dan di antara mereka ada orang yang mendengarkan (bacaanmu), padahal Kami telah meletakkan tutupan di atas hati mereka (sehingga mereka tidak) memahaminya dan (Kami letakkan) sumbatan di telinga mereka; dan jikapun mereka melihat segala tanda (kebenaran), mereka tetap tidak mau beriman kepadanya..." [Surah Al-An'am (6): 25]

[1.8 Keadaan Hati yang Mati]

Hati yang mati adalah hati yang telah kehilangan kekuatan hidup dan sensitivitasnya sehingga tidak lagi merespons perintah-perintah dan larangan-larangan Allah ﷻ. Pemiliknya tidak merasakan kepedihan dosa dan tidak merindukan kebaikan. Penyebab utama kematian hati adalah: jauh dari Al-Quran dan Sunnah, tenggelam dalam kemaksiatan, terlalu banyak bergaul dengan orang-orang yang lalai, dan sibuk dengan kelezatan-kelezatan dunia yang melenakan dari ingatan kepada Allah ﷻ.

Tanda-tanda hati yang mati antara lain: tidak merasa sedih atas ketidaktaatan kepada Allah ﷻ, tidak merasa gembira dengan ketaatan kepada-Nya, tidak terpengaruh oleh ayat-ayat Al-Quran, tidak merasa khawatir dengan kesempatan yang berlalu tanpa digunakan untuk beribadah, dan tidak merasa bersalah ketika melewatkan kewajiban-kewajiban agama.

Obat bagi hati yang mati adalah: bertobat dengan sungguh-sungguh kepada Allah ﷻ, memperbanyak membaca Al-Quran dengan perenungan, memperbanyak zikir kepada Allah ﷻ, berpuasa, salat malam, dan berteman dengan orang-orang saleh.
content_ar: > Part of a lengthy الدعاء reported by أحمد [3712], Abii ¥a‘la [q 1/156], at-Tabarani in a/-Kabir [3/74/1] and others. The الحديث is sahib. Refer to as-Sabibah [no. 199]. The wording of this الحديث of أحمد, غير أن, is in a singular gender not plural. [Translator’s Note] الله, the Exalted said, Aare SNS eRe Ste 5 RAGS see W354: BND 16 The example of those who disbelieve, is as that of him who shouts to the (flock of sheep) that hears nothing but calls and cries. (They are) deaf, dumb and blind. [seerah al-Baqarah (2) : 171] RAE TA CaA i o ae pest see aged And among them are some who listen to you, but can you make the deaf hear, حتى وإن they comprehend not? And among them are some who look at you, but can you guide the blind, حتى وإن they see not? [Sarab Yanus (10) : 42-43] ebes3 AE thes He dZat, spols 15155 pelSNe52 Mandl ES iy. ig YG oHI BAS GEERT CW GLATY, And of them are some who listen to you; but We have set veils on their القلوب, لذا they understand it not, and deafness in their ears; إذا they [1.8 The State of the Dead القلب] see every one of the signs they will not believe therein, to the point that عندما they come to argue with you, the disbelievers say: “These are nothing but tales of the men of old.” [Sarah al-An‘Gm (6) : 25] لذا He informed us that their القلوب cannot undefstand, and their ears cannot heat, and they do not believe in what they have seen of the Fire as He informed us about them عندما قال, aK WEL IL res xetaceiss Zope Cece PBZ ey we ASOD ASAD LL s Giles sdf ects And they say: ‘Our القلوب are under coverings from that which you invite us to, and in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a screen.’ [السورة Fussilat (41) : 5] لذا they mentioned the bartiers upon their القلوب, ears and eyes. Their bodies are alive - heating and seeing, but this is a الحياة of the الجسد devoid of الحياة in the القلب - like the الحياة of an animal - for the animals possess hearing and seeing, and eat and drink and marry. This is why الله said, Fea gee The example of those who disbelieve, is as that of him who shouts to the (flock of sheep) that heats nothing but calls and cries. [Sarab al-Bagarah (2) : 171} Likening them to the cattle, at whom the shepherd shouts, and they hear nothing except the cry (not الفهم what is said), as قال in other الآيات, rae “C2 404 Yhjahess pipes newts Orcas ce Or do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are only like cattle - nay, they are even farther astray from the الطريق. [Sarah al-Furgan (25) : 44] Sb Hiciieee Hesths ith z ssid aN NEA tetera oh fe 7% GDL sass (Padsijatch Lidikasdt, And indeed We have created many of the الجن and Mankind for النار. They have القلوب wherewith they understand not, they have eyes wherewith they hear not. They are like cattle - nay even more astray... Steg [seerah al-Araf (1) : 179] A group of the commentators, عندما referring to these الآيات and those that resembled them مثل the الآية, als ESE Gc hea G03 Gc oes Boe @ PAGS Bor Lepassigcies And عندما harm touches man, he invokes Us, lying down on his side, or sitting or standing. But عندما We have removed his harm from him, [1.8 The State of the Dead القلب] he passes on his الطريق as إذا he has never invoked Us for a harm that touched him! [Sarah Yunus (10) : 12] فيما يخص these and othet such الآيات that mention the faults of man and their condemnation, (the commentators) said, These الآيات refer to the disbelievers, and that the meaning of ‘man’ here is ‘the disbelievers.’ لذا the one who hears this explanation is left thinking that one who openly manifests الإسلام is not included in this condemnation and threat, rather his thoughts link (these الآيات) to those who openly manifested الشرك from amongst the Arabs, ot to those he knows to have openly shown disbelief مثل the Jews, the Christians and the polytheists of Turkey and India - and ومن ثَم he would not benefit from these الآيات that الله revealed لذا that His servants may be guided, لذا قيل in reply that firstly: those that openly manifest الإسلام include amongst them the المؤمن and hypocrite, and the hypocrites are many in all periods of time and they are in the lowest level of the النار. Secondly: man possesses a strain of hypocrisy and disbelief even إذا he possesses الإيمان along with this, as النبي ﷺ (8) said in the fadith reported by both البخاري and مسلم, There are four qualities which إذا found in a person make him a pure hypocrite, and the one who has a portion of them has a portion of hypocrisy حتى he leaves them: عندما he speaks he lies, عندما he is entrusted, he betrays, عندما he speaks, he speak a lie, عندما he makes a covenant, he proves treacherous, عندما he quarrels, he behaves in a الشر and insulting mannet ° لذا he informed us that the one who has a portion of these then he has a portion of hypocrisy, and ثبت in the Sabib of البخاري that قال to Aba Dhar, Indeed you have displayed a trait of Jahiliyyah in you,’ And Abi Dhar was from the most truthful of people with الاحترام to his الإيمان. He (4%) said in the authentic الأحاديث, Four (traits) in my الأمة are from the matters of Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic ignorance): boasting about noble descent, abusing the lineage, wailing [over the dead] and seeking rain from the stars. * You will indeed follow the ways of those that came قبل you, inch by inch such that إذا they were to enter a lizard hole, you أيضاً would do لذا. They asked, ° Sabih البخاري (Eng. Trans, 1/32 no. 33,] and صحيح مسلم (Eng, Trans. 1/40 no. 111). 7 Referring to the الحديث reported by al-Ma‘rar that, ‘At ar-Rabadha | met Abii Dhik (radiyAllahu ‘anha), who was wearing a cloak and likewise his slave. asked about the العقل for this. أجاب, ‘I abused a person by calling his الأم ‘with bad name,’ النبي ﷺ (4) said to me, ‘O Abii Dhar! Did you abuse him by calling his الأم with bad names? You have displayed .a trait of Jahiliyyah. Your slaves are yout brothers and الله has placed them under your command. لذا whoever has a الأخ under his command should feed him of what he eats and dress him of what he wears. Do not ask slaves to do things beyond their capacity and إذا you do لذا, then help them.’ صحيح البخاري [Eng, Trans. 1/29 no, 29] [Translator’s Note] * Sabih مسلم (Eng, Trans. 2/444 no. 2033}. [1.8 The State of the Dead القلب] ‘Do you mean the Jews and Christians?’ أجاب, ‘Who else?” What the early nations took to shall أيضاً be taken to by my nation, cubit by cubit and handspan by handspan. قالوا, ‘Do you mean the Persians and the Romans.’ To which he teplied, ‘Who else from amongst the people could it be.”"” Ibn Abi Mulaykah said, T met thirty of the الصحابة of محمد ﷺ (3) all of them fearing hypocrisy for themselves. " And from ‘علي - or Hudhayfah - that قال, The القلب ate of four types: the clear القلب that is illuminated by a torch - this is the القلب of the المؤمن. The encased القلب - this is the القلب of the disbeliever, the inverted القلب - this is the القلب of the hypocrite, and the القلب that has two attractions, a time عندما it is called to الإيمان, and a time عندما it is called to hypoctisy - these are a people that have mixed الخير actions with الشر ones. لذا عندما this is understood, it becomes known that every setvant benefits from what الله mentioned concetning الإيمان, either extolling the branches of الإيمان or censuring the branches of disbelief. ° Sabib البخاري (Eng, Trans. 9/314 no. 422], Sahib مسلم (Eng, Trans. 4/1402 no. 6448], أحمد [2/450]. ” Refer to Lgtidd الصراط al-Mustagim of ابن تيمية. " Reported by البخاري and مسلم The case mentioned above is similar to what some of them ask فيما يتعلق بـ His saying, Opes tai Guide us to the Straight الطريق. [Sarah al-Fatibah (1): 6] saying: ‘الله has already guided the المؤمن, لذا what benefit is there in seeking الهداية?’ Then some of them reply by saying that the meaning is ‘keep us firm upon الهداية’ as the Arab would say to the one who is asleep, ‘sleep حتى I come to you.’ Others from amongst them say that the meaning is, ‘keep our القلوب firm upon the الهداية’ and that the request for firmness has been omitted. Yet others from amongst them say that it means, ‘increase me in الهداية.’ This question really occurs بسبب the absence of their contemplating upon the Straight الطريق to which the servant seeks الهداية to, for the meaning [of the الآية] is [seeking الهداية to] act وفقاً لـ what الله ordered, and leave what He forbade in all matters. [1.9 The Need for Beneficial العلم] This is لأن the person, even إذا he has believed that محمد ﷺ is الرسول ﷺ of الله and that the القرآن الكريم is the الحق in a general الطريق, is commonly in need of العلم of that which would benefit him and harm him. He is in need of العلم فيما يتعلق بـ what he has been commanded to do and forbidden from doing in the finer aspects of the matters and in those areas of which he has no العلم. [Not only this but we find that] that which he does have العلم of, he does not put the greater part of it to practice! Assuming that all of [1.9 The Need for Beneficial العلم] the commands and prohibitions contained in the القرآن الكريم and السنة have reached him, then the Qut’an and السنة contain laws that are general and universal for which it is not possible to specify to every individual person - لذلك the person has been commanded بسبب the likes of this to ask for الهداية to the Straight الطريق. الهداية to the Straight الطريق includes all of the following matters: cognizance of what الرسول ﷺ (gg) came with in detail, cognizance of what comes under his general orders and concern for acting وفقاً لـ ones العلم, for indeed just having العلم is not a cause for attaining الهداية إذا one does not act وفقاً لـ his العلم. This is why قال to His النبي بعد the treaty of Hudaybiyyah,
